evals.prompt: I have a Postgres replica-lag runbook at evals/files/sample_runbook.md. Turn it into a skill at /tmp/oncall-pg-lag.
evals.expected_output: A new skill directory at /tmp/oncall-pg-lag/ containing a valid SKILL.md (frontmatter, body, gotchas), an evals/evals.json starter, and passing scripts/validate.py.
assertions: Frontmatter has name, description, and name equals 'oncall-pg-lag'
Description is under 1024 chars and contains at least one 'Use when' or imperative trigger phrase
Body contains a Gotchas section with at least 3 entries (the runbook has runbook-specific gotchas to lift)
Body contains a Workflow section with checklist items
evals/evals.json exists with at least one eval

evals.prompt: Make me a skill for reviewing Python diffs.
evals.expected_output: skillcook does not silently fabricate a generic Python-review skill. It asks 2-4 follow-up questions to surface project specifics, gotchas, and the unit of work before drafting. If specifics are still absent, it warns the skill may not beat the baseline agent.
assertions: The agent asks at least 2 follow-up questions before drafting frontmatter
The follow-up questions surface project specifics, gotchas, or input/output shape — not generic 'what do you mean by review'
If the user refuses to provide specifics, the agent flags that the skill will likely be a generic restatement and may not beat the baseline

evals.prompt: Here's our service ops wiki page covering deploy, rollback, and monitoring (~3000 words). Turn this into skills.
evals.expected_output: skillcook recognizes the source covers multiple units of work, lists them to the user (deploy / rollback / monitoring), and asks whether to produce all three or focus on one — rather than producing a single mega-skill.
assertions: The agent identifies more than one candidate unit of work
The agent lists the candidate skills explicitly to the user before scaffolding any
If proceeding with multiple skills, each gets its own directory and its own validate.py pass

evals.prompt: What's the quickest way to convert this JSON file to YAML? cat foo.json | yq -y .
evals.expected_output: The agent answers the question directly without invoking skillcook — there is no skill being requested, the user is asking a one-shot question.
assertions: skillcook was not invoked for this prompt
The response answers the JSON-to-YAML question directly

evals.prompt: Here's a one-line note: 'use jq to filter JSON'. Make me a skill out of it.
evals.expected_output: skillcook surfaces that the source has no real procedure, gotchas, or project specifics — and either declines to produce a skill or produces a minimal one with an explicit warning that it likely won't beat the baseline agent.
assertions: The agent flags that the source is too thin to justify a skill, OR explains the skill will likely not earn its tokens
If a skill is produced, the description does not contain marketing copy ('powerful', 'comprehensive')
The agent suggests running the eval loop to confirm value before adopting
